one_liner: Copper CRM offers a REST-based Developer API secured by API keys (with HMAC-signed requests) or OAuth 2.0, but API access is gated behind paid Professional/Business plans rather than being free/self-serve.

auth_methods.notes: Requires Authorization: ApiKey header plus X-Timestamp and X-Signature (HMAC-SHA256 of timestamp+method+path+body) headers on every request. Generated via System settings > API Keys in the Copper web app.
Authorization Code Grant flow producing a Bearer token; preferred for partner integrations rather than individual users.

self_serve_notes: No permanent free tier for API access; API access is gated to Professional and Business subscription plans. A 14-day free trial (no credit card required) is available on paid plans, which would allow a developer to self-serve an API key during the trial period.

api_surface.notes: Platform API covers CRM entities such as people, companies, opportunities, projects, tasks, and orders. Endpoint structure uses /platform/ prefix; docs include Getting Started and API reference for entities.

main_blocker: API access requires a paid Professional/Business subscription (though a no-credit-card 14-day trial can provide temporary self-serve access), and the API key auth scheme requires implementing HMAC-SHA256 request signing rather than simple bearer token auth.

evidence.supports: Describes both API Key and OAuth2.0 authentication methods for the Copper Developer API.
Details the required Authorization, X-Signature, and X-Timestamp headers and HMAC-SHA256 signature generation process for API key auth.
Documents OAuth2.0 Authorization Code Grant flow for partner integrations.
Indicates API access is gated behind paid Professional/Business plans with no permanent free tier, though trials are available.

flags: No official first-party MCP server confirmed; only third-party (ViaSocket, Zapier) and community MCP integrations found.
Self-serve API access effectively requires either a paid plan or use of the 14-day trial window.
HMAC request-signing adds implementation complexity beyond typical bearer-token APIs.
